ontology.foodon_gap_note: No specific FoodOn entry for Singaporean Teh-O; closest parent is 'black tea beverage' (FOODON_03301103). Teh-O is a local Singaporean preparation of black tea served without milk.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0EJW (Tea, black, infusion) is the closest parent. No specific code exists for Singaporean Teh-O preparation.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 227493005 'Black tea (substance)' is the closest parent concept. Teh-O as a Singaporean hawker beverage is not individually coded.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard cup of hot Teh-O as served at a Singapore hawker centre or coffee shop, approximately 200 ml, typically sweetened with sugar to taste.

nutrient_source.title: Tea, black, brewed, prepared with tap water (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, NDB No. 14355)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Teh-O is a Singaporean hawker-style black tea served without milk. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for brewed black tea (NDB 14355 / FDC ID 171917). The unsweetened brewed black tea values are used as the base; sugar addition is variable and consumer-dependent, so the record reflects the plain brewed tea without added sugar. HPB Singapore Foodo does not carry a dedicated Teh-O (plain, no milk) entry with full nutrient breakdown; USDA SR Legacy brewed black tea is the highest-quality approved source available for this preparation.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; plain brewed black tea contains no glycemic carbohydrates
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: Plain brewed black tea (Teh-O without sugar) contains no glycemic carbohydrates (carbohydrate_g = 0.0 per 100 g). The University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed GI tables confirm that unsweetened black tea has no measurable glycemic response. glycemic_index is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel value, effectively zero) per Project Veritas convention for zero-carbohydrate beverages. gi_category is 'low'. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 because carbohydrate content is 0 g per serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (3 mg per 100 g); no added salt
hypertension.guidance: Plain Teh-O without sugar is an appropriate beverage choice for individuals with hypertension due to its negligible sodium content; however, patients should be advised to limit or avoid added sugar, which is commonly added at hawker stalls.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero glycemic carbohydrates in unsweetened form; GI effectively zero
type2_diabetes.guidance: Unsweetened Teh-O has no glycemic impact and is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; patients must be counselled to request the beverage without added sugar ('kosong') as sugar addition at hawker stalls can contribute 10–20 g of simple carbohydrates per cup.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero fat, zero saturated fat, zero cholesterol; black tea flavonoids may confer modest cardioprotective benefit
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Plain Teh-O is a heart-friendly beverage with no fat, saturated fat, or cholesterol; epidemiological evidence suggests regular consumption of unsweetened black tea is associated with modest reductions in cardiovascular risk, though patients should avoid sweetened versions to prevent excess caloric and glycemic load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Hot Teh-O is a traditional Singaporean hawker-centre and coffee-shop (kopitiam) beverage consisting of hot brewed black tea served without milk. The name derives from the Hokkien word 'teh' (tea) and the suffix 'O' indicating the absence of milk, distinguishing it from 'Teh' (tea with condensed milk) and 'Teh-C' (tea with evaporated milk). The tea is typically brewed using a blend of Ceylon and Assam black tea leaves strained through a cloth sock filter, producing a strong, amber-coloured infusion with a robust, slightly astringent flavour. It is served hot and may be sweetened with white sugar or rock sugar to taste; the unsweetened version is called 'Teh-O Kosong'. Nutritionally, plain unsweetened Teh-O is essentially calorie-free (approximately 1 kcal per 100 ml), contains no fat, no protein, and no glycemic carbohydrates, making it one of the most diet-friendly beverages available in the Singaporean hawker context. It contains trace amounts of potassium (37 mg/100 g), magnesium (3 mg/100 g), and riboflavin. Black tea is a source of polyphenolic flavonoids including theaflavins and thearubigins, which have been associated with antioxidant and modest cardioprotective effects in epidemiological studies. The beverage is suitable for vegetarians, vegans, and individuals following halal and gluten-free diets. For individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease, the unsweetened form is clinically appropriate; the key dietary risk is the common practice of adding sugar at the point of service, which can significantly alter the glycemic and caloric profile. Teh-O is priced at approximately SGD 1.00–2.00 at hawker centres and kopitiams and is one of the most widely consumed hot beverages in Singapore.
common_user_queries: Is Teh-O the same as black tea?
How many calories in hot Teh-O?
What is the difference between Teh and Teh-O?
Can I drink Teh-O if I have high blood pressure?
How is Teh-O made in Singapore?

confidence_reason: Teh-O is a well-defined Singaporean hawker beverage preparation of plain black tea without milk. HPB Singapore Foodo does not carry a dedicated full-nutrient entry for plain Teh-O; values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy brewed black tea (FDC ID 171917), which is an approved secondary source and a highly appropriate analogue. The adaptation is transparent and defensible. Confidence is 'medium' rather than 'high' because the exact HPB-sourced nutrient profile for this specific hawker preparation is not available.
notes: 1. energy_kcal is 1.0 kcal per 100 g (not zero) per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for brewed black tea; this reflects the negligible but non-zero energy from trace organic compounds in brewed tea. 2. protein_g is naturally zero — plain brewed black tea contains no protein by nature. 3. fat_g is naturally zero — plain brewed black tea contains no fat by nature. 4. carbohydrate_g is naturally zero — unsweetened brewed black tea contains no glycemic carbohydrates; any sugar is added at point of service and is not included in the base nutrient profile. 5. sugar_g is naturally zero for the unsweetened preparation documented here. 6. dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero for brewed tea (fibre remains in the leaf solids). 7. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per Project Veritas convention for zero-carbohydrate beverages. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 since carbohydrate_g is 0. 8. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for brewed black tea. 9. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for brewed black tea. 10. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for brewed black tea. 11. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, vitamin_d_mcg are confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for brewed black tea. 12. The record reflects the plain unsweetened preparation (Teh-O Kosong equivalent); sugar addition is consumer-dependent and not included. 13. Source: field survey data (Dapur Penyet - Funan) preserved as provenance context only; nutrient data sourced from USDA FoodData Central as per approved source policy.
